sentences.english: Hi Rachel, it's Kevin. I'm really sorry, but I don't think I can come in today. I woke up with a terrible headache and a fever.
sentences.korean: 안녕 레이첼, 케빈이에요. 정말 죄송한데 오늘 출근 못 할 것 같아요. 심한 두통과 열이 있어서요.

sentences.english: Oh no, I'm sorry to hear that. Do you think it's the flu?
It might be. I'm going to see if I can get an appointment with my doctor this afternoon.
sentences.korean: 그럴 수도 있어요. 오늘 오후에 의사 예약을 잡을 수 있는지 볼게요.

sentences.english: Of course. Take care of yourself first. Do you have anything urgent on your plate today?
sentences.korean: 물론이죠. 먼저 몸부터 챙기세요. 오늘 급한 업무가 있나요?

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Philosophy of Science — The Demarcation Problem

sentences.english: I was supposed to send the monthly report to the marketing team by noon. I've got about eighty percent of it done. Would it be possible for someone to finish it?
sentences.korean: 정오까지 마케팅팀에 월간 보고서를 보내기로 했는데, 약 80% 정도 완성했어요.

sentences.english: Sure, I'll ask Lisa to take over. Is the file on the shared drive?
sentences.korean: 네, 리사에게 넘기라고 할게요. 파일이 공유 드라이브에 있나요?

sentences.english: Yes, it's in the April folder. I'll send Lisa a quick email with notes on what's left.
sentences.korean: 네, 4월 폴더에 있어요. 리사에게 남은 작업 메모와 함께 간단히 이메일 보낼게요.

sentences.english: Perfect. Don't worry about it. Just rest up and let me know how you're feeling tomorrow.
sentences.korean: 좋아요. 걱정 마세요. 푹 쉬시고 내일 상태 알려주세요.

sentences.english: Thanks, Rachel. I really appreciate it.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Panel Discussion — AI and the Future of Work
TED-Style Talk — The Science of Habits
